title: Studies on Pollutants : A New Reagent for the Determination of Nitrite in Water
author: (Miss) ALKA BHATT
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Ravisbankar University, Raipur-492 010 (M.P.) 

  Manuscript received 24 January 1980, accepted 19 May 1980  

 A new reagent system is described for the micro determination of nitrites in water. The method is based on the reaction of catechol and micro amount of nitrite in 7-8  M  acetic acid and coupling the resultant compound with  p -aminopbenol to form a violet coloured solution. It exhibits a wavelength of maximum absorption at 530 nm. The Beer's law is obeyed from 0.6 ppm to 1.6 ppm. The molar absorptivity and Sandell's sensitivity are found to be 2.3 x 10 4  litre mole -1  cm -1  and 0.0023  µ g cm -2  respectively.
